title: Tapered Freestanding Ladder Bookcase
summary: A symmetric leaning-ladder silhouette, narrower at the top than the bottom, built as two matching 10°-angled side frames joined by shelves and a rear stretcher — no wall lean required.
description:
Unlike a bookshelf that leans its front legs against a wall, both sides of this ladder taper inward at the same 10° angle from bottom to top, and a 2x2 stretcher across the back ties the two side frames together so the whole piece stands entirely on its own. Start by cutting each side's outer leg trim at 68-1/2" with both ends at 10°, then cut the shorter inner trim pieces — bottom, between-shelf, and top — all at 10° on both ends, cut parallel to each other.

Nail the inner trim pieces to the outer leg trim with a 3/4" gap between each one, which works out to a bit over 1-1/2" at the very top — **this gap spacing is what sets where each shelf sits**, so get it consistent across both side frames before moving on. Build both side frames identically.

Fit the shelves into the gaps between the inner trim pieces on both frames and nail through the outer legs into the shelves. Since the frame tapers, the inside top pieces that cap each frame are cut to two different lengths — wider at 22-1/2" and narrower at 19-1/2" — mitered to match the 10° taper rather than square-cut. **The 2x2 stretcher across the back and the corner blocks at the top corners are what make this genuinely freestanding** rather than a leaning shelf that needs a wall to hold it upright — don't skip them even if the piece feels stable enough during dry-fitting.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 10° off square, parallel to each other.
Short point to short point; both ends cut at 10°, NOT parallel.
Short point to short point; both ends cut at 10°, NOT parallel.
Short point to short point measurement.

steps.title: Cut the leg trim
steps.body: Cut the outer leg trim pieces at 10° on both ends. Cut the bottom, between-shelf, and top inner trim pieces, all at 10° on both ends, cut parallel to each other.

steps.title: Build the side frames
steps.body: Nail the inner trim pieces to an outer leg trim piece, leaving a 3/4" gap between each one, which works out to a bit over 1-1/2" at the top. Build two identical side frames.

steps.title: Fit the shelves
steps.body: Fit the shelves into the gaps left between the inner trim pieces on both side frames. Nail through the outer legs into the shelves with glue.

steps.title: Cap the tops
steps.body: Cut and fit the inside top trim pieces between the top two legs of each frame — the wide pieces on one frame, the narrow pieces on the other, mitered to match the taper. Nail in place with glue.

steps.title: Add the top corner blocks
steps.body: Predrill from the outside of the legs and attach the top corner blocks, keeping outside edges flush.

steps.title: Add the back stretcher
steps.body: Attach the back stretcher between the two side frames to tie them together and make the piece freestanding.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Tapered freestanding ladder bookcase with five shelves
